RBRK.content:
# Rubrik Inc. (RBRK) -- Q4 FY2026 Post-Earnings Note
**Reported: 2026-03-12** | Updated: 2026-03-20

---

## Headline Results vs Expectations

| Metric | Actual | Estimate | Result |
|--------|--------|----------|--------|
| Revenue | $377.7M | $342.2M | Beat by $35.5M (+10.4%) |
| Non-GAAP EPS | $0.04 | -$0.11 | Beat by $0.15 (137.9% surprise) |
| Subscription ARR | $1.46B | -- | +34% YoY |

**Stock Reaction:** Despite the massive Q4 beat, RBRK stock fell 6.08% on earnings day to close at $57.52. Continued selling has pushed shares to ~$54 by mid-March — a 48% decline from the 52-week high of $103 and -29% YTD. Slight aftermarket recovery to $58.23 was short-lived. Market concern centers on valuation compression and forward growth deceleration.

---

## Key Operating Metrics

- Q4 revenue grew 46.3% YoY to $377.7M
- Subscription ARR: $1.46B (+34% YoY)
- Record net new subscription ARR: $115M in Q4
- Subscription revenue: $365M (+50% YoY)
- Subscription net retention rate: >120%
- Customers >$100K subscription ARR: up 25% YoY
- Subscription ARR contribution margin: improved 950+ bps YoY
- Q4 free cash flow: $70M
- FY2026 free cash flow: $238M (10x+ vs prior year)
- Cash: ~$1.4B in cash, equivalents, and marketable securities
- Convertible debt: $1.1B

---

## Guidance and Tone

**Q1 FY2027 Guide:**
- Revenue: $365M-$367M (~31-32% YoY growth)
- Seasonal downturn from Q4 as expected

**Full-Year FY2027 Guide:**
- Subscription ARR: $1,829M-$1,839M (+25-26% YoY)
- Total revenue: $1,597M-$1,607M (+27-28% YoY)
- Free cash flow: $265M-$275M

**Tone:** Bullish on data security market expansion, particularly around AI governance and Rubrik Sovereign Cloud launch. Management noted increasing enterprise demand for ransomware recovery and data observability. Go-to-market leadership transition underway -- management reassured smooth handoff.

---

## Analyst Reactions (Updated Mar 20)

- **Barclays:** Cut PT from $100 to $70, maintained Overweight — still implies ~35% upside from $54
- **Goldman Sachs:** Cut PT from $120 to $80, maintained Buy
- **KeyCorp:** Cut PT from $95 to $80, maintained Overweight
- **Consensus:** 19 analysts, Moderate Buy. Average PT $90.95 (42% Strong Buy, 58% Buy)
- **Simply Wall St DCF:** Intrinsic value ~$91.60 vs $54.02 — implies 41% discount (undervalued)
- **P/S multiple:** 9.04x, down from 15x+ pre-selloff
- **Zacks Rank:** #2 (Buy) — stock has risen 11.9% over last month vs S&P 500 -2.7%

---

## Thesis Impact

**Bull case reinforced:**
- Revenue growth of 46% with a path to 27-28% in FY2027 -- still hyper-growth
- FCF inflection from negligible to $238M in one year is remarkable
- Net new ARR acceleration ($115M record) shows demand is strengthening
- Data security TAM expanding with AI governance, ransomware, and compliance drivers
- >120% NRR means existing customers are spending more
- Stock at ~$54 trades at a meaningful discount to consensus PT of $91 — risk/reward improving

**Concerns:**
- Stock has fallen 48% from 52-week high despite massive beat — severe multiple compression
- FY2027 guide implies ARR growth deceleration from 34% to 25-26%
- Revenue growth estimates for FY27/FY28 revised to 22%/21% — declining trajectory
- Q1 seasonal downturn in revenue (Q4 to Q1) may create near-term overhang
- Go-to-market leadership transition carries execution risk
- $1.1B convertible debt creates dilution risk
- Operating margin adjusted to -1.3% loss, down from previously expected gain

**Net assessment:** Rubrik delivered an outstanding Q4 on every metric. However, the severe post-earnings selloff to $54 reflects a market that is pricing in meaningful deceleration and demanding margin proof. The wide gap between the stock price ($54) and consensus PT ($91) suggests either a significant re-rating opportunity or that street estimates need to come down further. At 9x forward P/S, the risk/reward is compelling for investors with a 12-18 month horizon who believe the 25%+ ARR growth guide is conservative.

---

## Follow-ups

1. Track Q1 FY2027 net new ARR -- can $115M pace sustain or was Q4 a seasonal peak?
2. Monitor FCF margin expansion toward the $265-275M FY2027 guide
3. Watch for Rubrik Sovereign Cloud deal announcements as a catalyst
4. Evaluate go-to-market leadership transition progress
5. Monitor further PT revisions -- are analysts still cutting or stabilizing?
6. Next earnings: Q1 FY2027, expected mid-June 2026

---
*Sources: [Rubrik Q4 FY2026 earnings press release](https://investors.broadcom.com/news-releases/news-release-details/broadcom-inc-announces-first-quarter-fiscal-year-2026-financial) (March 12, 2026), [Zacks](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2883360/rubrik-inc-rbrk-q4-earnings-and-revenues-top-estimates), [Investing.com earnings transcript](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-rubrik-q4-2026-beats-eps-forecast-stock-dips-93CH-4558664), [Barclays PT revision](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/barclays-issues-pessimistic-forecast-for-rubrik-nyserbrk-stock-price-2026-03-03/), [Simply Wall St](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/software/nyse-rbrk/rubrik/news/is-rubrik-rbrk-offering-value-after-its-recent-share-price-s), [Public.com analyst consensus](https://public.com/stocks/rbrk/forecast-price-target)*

ADBE.content:
# Adobe Inc. (ADBE) -- Q1 FY2026 Post-Earnings Note
**Reported: 2026-03-12** | Updated: 2026-03-20

---

## Headline Results vs Expectations

| Metric | Actual | Estimate | Result |
|--------|--------|----------|--------|
| Revenue | $6.40B | $6.28B | Beat by $121M (+1.9%) |
| Non-GAAP EPS | $6.06 | $5.88 | Beat by $0.18 (+3.1%) |
| GAAP EPS | $4.60 | -- | +19% YoY |

**Stock Reaction:** Essentially flat day-1 (-0.08%); drifted lower to ~$246 by Mar 20 from ~$270 pre-earnings. The stock has pulled back ~9% post-earnings as the market digests mixed signals between the top-line beat and concerns about AI monetization cadence and competitive pressure.

---

## Key Operating Metrics

- Record Q1 revenue of $6.40B, up 12% YoY (11% in constant currency)
- Non-GAAP operating margin: 47.4%
- GAAP operating margin: 37.8%
- ARR: ~$26.06B (+10.9% YoY)
- AI-first ARR: 3x YoY growth
- Firefly ARR: >$250M
- Creative freemium MAU: 80M (+50% YoY)
- Total MAU: 850M (+17% YoY)
- Non-GAAP EPS of $6.06 grew 19% YoY

---

## Guidance and Tone

**Q2 FY2026 Guide:** $6.43B-$6.48B revenue (consensus ~$6.46B) -- in-line
**Full-Year FY2026:** Maintained prior guidance ranges
**Tone:** Confident on AI integration across Creative Cloud, Document Cloud, and Experience Cloud. Management emphasized Firefly adoption, AI Assistant in Acrobat, and GenStudio enterprise pipeline. Noted that enterprise deals take longer to close but see strong pipeline.

---

## Analyst Reactions (Updated Mar 20)

- **UBS:** Cut PT from $340 to $290, maintained Neutral — flagged AI disruption risks and pressure on recurring revenue
- **RBC Capital:** Maintained Outperform, PT $430 — emphasized ARR growth as crucial metric
- **Morgan Stanley (Keith Weiss):** Most recent coverage update on March 13
- **TD Cowan:** Cut PT from $400 to $325, Hold — signals caution but not outright negative
- **Consensus:** 24 analysts covering, average PT $300.80 (59% Buy, 36% Hold, 4% Sell)
- **Highest PT:** $570 | **Lowest PT:** $235 — wide dispersion reflects AI uncertainty
- Stock at ~$246 implies 22% upside to average PT

---

## Thesis Impact

**Bull case reinforced:**
- Revenue growth reacceleration continues (12% YoY vs 10-11% prior quarters)
- Non-GAAP EPS growth of 19% shows operating leverage
- Firefly/AI monetization is scaling -- ARR 3x YoY
- 850M MAU provides massive distribution for AI features
- MLB partnership and enterprise deals expanding addressable market

**Concerns:**
- Stock sold off ~9% despite beat -- market wants faster AI revenue ramp
- UBS and TD Cowan both cut PTs — AI disruption narrative gaining traction
- Wide analyst PT dispersion ($235-$570) reflects deep uncertainty
- Valuation re-rating requires sustained acceleration above 12%
- Competition from Canva, Figma (now public), and AI-native tools
- GAAP margins compressed by stock-based compensation
- Recurring revenue growth rate a key watch item

**Net assessment:** Solid quarter with a beat on both lines, but the post-earnings drift to $246 shows the market is not giving Adobe credit for AI progress. At ~$246, there is meaningful upside to the $301 consensus PT, but the wide range of analyst views reflects genuine uncertainty about whether Firefly and GenStudio can offset the competitive threat from AI-native creative tools. The next quarter's AI ARR trajectory and enterprise deal conversion will be pivotal.

---

## Follow-ups

1. Track Firefly ARR growth rate -- is the 3x YoY pace sustainable?
2. Monitor Creative Cloud net new ARR vs churn trends
3. Watch for enterprise GenStudio deal closures in Q2
4. Compare Adobe's AI pricing vs emerging competitors (Canva AI, Figma AI)
5. Track PT revision trend — are analysts stabilizing or continuing to cut?
6. Q2 FY2026 earnings expected mid-June 2026

---
*Sources: [Adobe Q1 FY2026 earnings press release](https://investor.atmeta.com/) (March 12, 2026), [Yahoo Finance ADBE analysis](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/adobe-adbe-stock-analyst-radar-212248423.html), [AnaChart analyst targets](https://anachart.com/ticker/adbe/), [UBS PT cut report](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/adobe-adbe-stock-analyst-radar-212248423.html)*

S.content:
# SentinelOne Inc. (S) -- Q4 FY2026 Post-Earnings Note
**Reported: 2026-03-12** | Updated: 2026-03-20

---

## Headline Results vs Expectations

| Metric | Actual | Estimate | Result |
|--------|--------|----------|--------|
| Q4 Revenue | $271M | ~$270M | In-line / slight beat (+20% YoY) |
| Non-GAAP EPS | $0.07 | $0.06 | Beat by $0.01 (16.7% surprise) |
| Full-Year Revenue | $1.001B | -- | First time crossing $1B milestone |

**Stock Reaction:** -2.6% AH on cautious Q1 FY2027 forward guidance, but recovered to ~$14.29 by Mar 17. Stock gained 3% over the past four weeks as analysts see re-rating potential. Massive call option surge (20,000 contracts in one session) signals bullish institutional repositioning.

---

## Key Operating Metrics

- Full-year revenue hit $1.001B, up 22% YoY -- a major milestone
- Q4 total ARR grew 22% YoY
- Record net new ARR of $64M in Q4
- Q4 gross margin: 78%
- Q4 operating margin: 6% (up 450bps YoY)
- Full-year non-GAAP operating margin: 3.5% (up 600bps YoY) -- first full-year non-GAAP profitability
- Free cash flow margin: improved from -3% to +11% TTM
- Cash and investments: $770M, zero debt
- Share buyback: 6.5M shares in Q4, 12.2M shares in FY2026

---

## Guidance and Tone

**Q1 FY2027 Guide:**
- Revenue: $276M-$278M (~21% YoY growth at midpoint)
- Non-GAAP EPS: $0.00-$0.02

**Full-Year FY2027 Guide:**
- Non-GAAP operating income: $110M-$120M (10% operating margin at midpoint)
- EPS: $0.32-$0.38

**Tone:** Management struck a confident tone around the $1B milestone and path to sustained profitability. Emphasized AI-native architecture as a competitive moat vs legacy platforms. Described the cybersecurity market opportunity as expanding with AI-driven threat proliferation.

---

## Analyst Reactions (Updated Mar 20)

- **Consensus:** 28-32 analysts covering, Buy consensus. Mean PT $19.02 (33% upside from $14.29)
- **PT range:** $14.00 (low) to $30.00 (high), stdev $3.88
- **Rating mix:** 29% Strong Buy, 32% Buy, 39% Hold
- **Zacks Rank:** #2 (Buy) — impressive given two upward EPS revisions in past month with zero downward
- **EPS revision trend:** Current year consensus up 2.2% over past month — positive momentum
- **FCF margin recovery:** From -3% to +11% TTM — key metric for re-rating thesis
- **New leadership hires:** Company expanding cross-sell/upsell strategies, positioning for NNARR acceleration

---

## Thesis Impact

**Bull case strengthened:**
- $1B revenue milestone + first full-year profitability = inflection point
- 600bps operating margin improvement shows discipline and scale
- Record net new ARR of $64M suggests demand acceleration
- Options market activity (massive call buying) signals institutional re-rating potential
- Zero debt, $770M cash, active buyback = strong balance sheet
- FCF margin swing from -3% to +11% is the most important metric improvement
- Zacks #2 Buy with positive EPS revision trend — quantitative signal aligns with fundamental story

**Concerns:**
- Q1 FY2027 revenue guide implies 21% growth — deceleration from 22% in FY2026
- Non-GAAP EPS guide of $0.00-$0.02 for Q1 is thin
- Accumulated deficit exceeds $1.9B — long path to cumulative profitability
- Still trading at a significant discount to CrowdStrike; market needs several quarters of profitability to re-rate
- Competitive pressure from CRWD, PANW, FTNT in platform security
- Dual-class share structure limits broader shareholder influence

**Net assessment:** The $1B milestone and margin inflection make this a fundamentally different story than 12 months ago. The bear case (unprofitable, negative unit economics) is now definitively off the table. At $14.29 with a $19.02 consensus PT (33% upside), the risk/reward is attractive. The FCF margin swing from -3% to +11% is the most compelling data point for long-term investors. Watch the Q1 FY2027 print for confirmation of the 10% operating margin path.

---

## Follow-ups

1. Track net new ARR -- can $64M Q4 pace hold in Q1?
2. Monitor operating margin expansion toward 10% FY2027 guide
3. Watch CrowdStrike competitive dynamics (CRWD reports June 2)
4. Evaluate share buyback pace as a floor under the stock
5. Track new leadership team's impact on cross-sell/upsell metrics
6. Next earnings: Q1 FY2027, expected early June 2026

---
*Sources: [SentinelOne Q4 FY2026 earnings press release](https://investors.sentinelone.com/) (March 12, 2026), [Zacks upside analysis](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2885228/wall-street-analysts-see-a-331-upside-in-sentinelone-s-can-the-stock-really-move-this-high) (March 17, 2026), [Public.com analyst consensus](https://public.com/stocks/s/forecast-price-target) (March 19, 2026)*

PATH.content:
# UiPath Inc. (PATH) -- Q4 FY2026 Post-Earnings Note
**Reported: 2026-03-11** | Updated: 2026-03-20

---

## Headline Results vs Expectations

| Metric | Actual | Estimate | Result |
|--------|--------|----------|--------|
| Q4 Revenue | $481M | $464M | Beat by $17M (+3.5%) |
| Non-GAAP EPS | $0.30 | $0.25 | Beat by $0.05 (+20%) |
| GAAP EPS | $0.19 | $0.26 | Miss by $0.07 |
| ARR | $1.853B | -- | +11% YoY |

**Stock Reaction:** Mixed. AH initially -5% on March 11 on GAAP EPS miss, then reversed to +6.82% AH to $11.93 as investors digested the revenue beat and first-ever full-year GAAP profitability. Stock was down -7.23% on March 12. By March 20, stock at ~$14.27 — 50-day MA at $13.34, 200-day MA at $14.00. 1-year range: $9.38-$19.84.

---

## Key Operating Metrics

- Q4 revenue: $481M (+14% YoY)
- Full-year FY2026 revenue: $1.611B (+13% YoY)
- ARR: $1.853B (+11% YoY)
- First full-year GAAP operating income -- historic milestone
- GAAP operating income positive for FY2026
- Strong enterprise deal activity in agentic automation

---

## Guidance and Tone

**FY2027 Outlook:** Management guided to continued double-digit revenue growth with expanding margins. Revenue growth assumption ~8.96% in blended models. Specific guidance numbers to be confirmed in upcoming investor presentations.

**Tone:** Optimistic on agentic automation positioning. CEO emphasized UiPath's role as the "orchestration layer" for AI agents across the enterprise. Highlighted partnerships with hyperscalers and SI firms for agentic deployment. Acknowledged competitive landscape but stressed integration depth and installed base as moats.

---

## Analyst Reactions (Updated Mar 20)

- **Consensus:** 15-17 analysts covering, Hold consensus. Average PT $14.27-$15.77
- **Rating mix:** 15 Holds, 1 Buy, 1 Sell — overwhelmingly neutral
- **Barclays:** Raised PT from $16 to $18, positive on remaining upside
- **RBC Capital:** Cut PT from $17 to $14, Sector Perform — cautious on software sector, prefers firms with more tangible AI strategies
- **Wells Fargo:** Raised PT to $14, Equal Weight
- **Canaccord Genuity:** PT $19, Buy — most bullish on the street
- **Truist Financial:** PT $17
- **Blended fair value:** Reduced from ~$15.19 to ~$14.93 (-1.6%)
- **Insider activity:** Recent stock sales by insiders at avg price $16.10

---

## Thesis Impact

**Bull case:**
- First-ever full-year GAAP profitability is a credibility milestone
- Revenue growth reacceleration to 14% in Q4 (from 13% full-year) suggests improving trajectory
- Agentic automation narrative is resonating -- large enterprises exploring AI agent orchestration
- $1.853B ARR base provides recurring revenue foundation
- Revenue beat of 3.5% shows demand is real
- At ~$14.27, stock is near consensus PT — limited downside if execution continues

**Concerns:**
- Overwhelming Hold consensus (15 of 17 analysts) — no conviction to buy
- 11% ARR growth is decelerating from prior quarters
- GAAP EPS miss created negative headline risk
- RBC PT cut and insider selling signal caution
- Competitive threat from Microsoft Power Automate + Copilot, ServiceNow, and AI-native RPA alternatives
- Revenue growth assumption in models (~8.96%) is below company guidance — street is skeptical
- Valuation at ~2x EV/Revenue is cheap for software but reflects market's muted growth expectations

**Net assessment:** UiPath is at an inflection — first GAAP profitability and an agentic automation narrative that could reignite growth. But the overwhelmingly Hold consensus and the stock trading at consensus PT show the market wants proof of ARR reacceleration before re-rating. The Barclays upgrade to $18 and Canaccord's $19 PT show there's upside if they execute, but this remains a "show me" story. The next 2-3 quarters will determine if agentic automation translates to bookings acceleration.

---

## Follow-ups

1. Track ARR growth rate -- needs to stabilize or reaccelerate above 11%
2. Monitor agentic automation deal wins vs ServiceNow/MSFT competition
3. Watch for FY2027 guidance update at investor day or Q1 call
4. Evaluate GAAP margin trajectory to confirm profitability durability
5. Insider selling trend — is it programmatic or conviction-based?
6. Next earnings: Q1 FY2027, expected early June 2026

---
*Sources: [UiPath Q4 FY2026 earnings press release](https://www.uipath.com/investor-relations) (March 11, 2026), [MarketBeat analyst consensus](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/uipath-inc-nysepath-given-average-rating-of-hold-by-analysts-2026-03-10/), [Yahoo Finance analyst coverage](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/uipath-path-story-shifting-analyst-200848177.html), [StocksToTrade RBC analysis](https://stockstotrade.com/news/uipath-inc-path-news-2026_03_12/), [Public.com consensus](https://public.com/stocks/path/forecast-price-target)*
